infobox.value: ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): ระนอง) is a town (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")*) in southern [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), capital of the [Ranong Province](/wiki/Ranong_Province "Ranong Province") and the [Mueang Ranong District](/wiki/Mueang_Ranong_District "Mueang Ranong District"). The town covers completely the area of the *[tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")* Khao Niwet (เขานิเวศน์). As of 2024, it had a population of 16,909. Ranong lies 586 kilometres (364 mi) south-southwest of Bangkok by road.

**Ranong** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): ระนอง) is a town (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")*) in southern [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), capital of the [Ranong Province](/wiki/Ranong_Province "Ranong Province") and the [Mueang Ranong District](/wiki/Mueang_Ranong_District "Mueang Ranong District"). The town covers completely the area of the *[tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")* Khao Niwet (เขานิเวศน์). As of 2024, it had a population of 16,909. Ranong lies 586 kilometres (364 mi) south-southwest of Bangkok by road.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Ranong is on the estuary of the [Pak Chan (or Kraburi) River](/wiki/Kraburi_River "Kraburi River"), opposite [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar")'s [Kawthaung](/wiki/Kawthaung "Kawthaung") (formerly Victoria Point). The [Tenasserim Hills](/wiki/Tenasserim_Hills "Tenasserim Hills") rise directly to the east of Ranong, and another small ridge runs along the edge of the estuary to the town's north. Neighboring subdistricts are (from north clockwise) Bang Non, Hat Som Paen, Bang Rin and Pak Nam.[[3]](#cite_note-CONTACT-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

On 14 March 1936 Ranong sanitary district was elevated to subdistrict municipality.[[4]](#cite_note-HISTORY-4)

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Administration")]

### Central government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Central government")]

The administration of Ranong town is responsible for Khao Niwet subdistrict (*[tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")*) with 4.2 square kilometres (1.6 sq mi) and 16,909 people of 8,333 households. Khao Niwet subdistrict is not divided into villages (*[mubans](/wiki/Muban "Muban")*).

### Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Local government")]

Ranong town municipality (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")*) covers the whole Khao Niwet subdistrict.

## Healthcare

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Healthcare")]

### Hospital

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Hospital")]

There is [general hospital](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Thailand#General_Hospitals_(Category_S_and_M1) "List of hospitals in Thailand") in Ranong with 300 beds.

### Health promoting hospital

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Health promoting hospital")]

There is no [health-promoting hospital](/wiki/Hospitals_in_Thailand#Sub-district_Health_Promoting_Hospitals "Hospitals in Thailand") in the town.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Education")]

There are two schools in the town:

* Ban Khao Niwet municipal school.[[5]](#cite_note-BKNMS-5)
* Wat Upanantharam municipal school.[[6]](#cite_note-WUMS-6)

## Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Religion")]

There are two active temples, where [Theravada Buddhism](/wiki/Theravada_Buddhism "Theravada Buddhism") is practised by local residents:[[7]](#cite_note-MAHANIKAI-7)

| Temple name | Thai |
| --- | --- |
| Wat Suwankhiri | วัดสุวรรณคีรี |
| Wat Uppanantharam | วัดอุปนันทาราม |

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Climate")]

Ranong has a [tropical monsoon climate](/wiki/Tropical_monsoon_climate "Tropical monsoon climate") ([Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *Am*). There is little variation in the temperature throughout the year, although the pre-monsoon months (February to April) are somewhat hotter in the day. However, Ranong's position to the west of the Tenasserim Hills means that the [monsoon](/wiki/Monsoon "Monsoon") season's rains are greatly amplified, resulting in torrential rains from May until October, and significant rainfall in the transition months of April and November.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ranong&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Transportation")]

[Phet Kasem Road](/wiki/Phet_Kasem_Road "Phet Kasem Road") (Thailand Route 4) runs through the city. [Ranong Airport](/wiki/Ranong_Airport "Ranong Airport") is about 24 kilometres (15 mi) south of town.

The [Port Authority of Thailand](/wiki/Port_Authority_of_Thailand "Port Authority of Thailand") operates the Ranong Port, which is Thailand's principal [Indian Ocean](/wiki/Indian_Ocean "Indian Ocean") port. In 2008, the [Ranong human-smuggling incident](/wiki/Ranong_human-smuggling_incident "Ranong human-smuggling incident") resulted in 54 deaths.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Several ferries are connecting to [Kawthaung](/wiki/Kawthaung "Kawthaung"), Myanmar, which can be accessed by foreigners without visa. There also is a boat from Ranong to the Burmese resort island [Thahtay Kyun](/wiki/Thahtay_Kyun "Thahtay Kyun").
